channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Xbox and Netflix 'kicking around' ideas for subscription bundles
item.content:encoded:
Xbox and Netflix have "kicked around ideas" for subscription bundles that could potentially pair Xbox Game Pass with the popular entertainment streaming service.

During a recent conversation with The Information, Netflix co-CEO Greg Peters explained he has met with newly-appointed Xbox boss Asha Sharma multiple times since she took charge in February. 

Both companies have already partnered on a number of occasions—for instance, they are currently working together on an upcoming Netflix animated series based on Minecraft—and now it seems like future plans could include Xbox Game Pass.

Peters said he and Sharma have "kicked around ideas" for how their respective companies could partner on subscription bundles and noted they "wouldn't eliminate any possibilities." That said, there are currently no concrete plans for a Netflix-Game Pass deal.

"You would have to do it in a way that works for the consumer and works for both companies, and frankly, I think Microsoft's still trying to figure out how to make the Game Pass bundle work for Microsoft," Peters said. "But what I like about Asha's thinking is, it's all about, how do we do more? And it's already been exciting to watch."

Related:Epic Games layoffs serve as a timely reminder that accountability is dead

The Information also claims Sharma is considering revamping the pricing structure of Xbox Game Pass to offer lower-priced tiers.

Microsoft reworked Xbox Game Pass' pricing tiers in October 2025 to "offer more flexibility, choice, and value to all players." As part of that move, the cost of the 'Ultimate' tier was raised to $29.99/month from $19.99/month.

Xbox is currently undergoing a series of leadership changes. Along with Spencer's retirement, former Xbox president Sarah Bond resigned. In the past couple of days, two more execs followed suit: VP of partnerships, business development, and marketing, Lori Wright; and general manager of gaming AI at Xbox, Haiyan Zhang.

As for Netflix, Peters explained the streamer's video game division will focus on bringing cloud-based titles to television screens in 2026. According to the co-CEO, Netflix Games will be expanding its "cloud-first strategy" over the next year with the addition of new titles such as the recently-announced soccer simulator from Delphi Interactive and FIFA.

About the Author

Contributing Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Diego Nicolás Argüello is a freelance journalist and critic from Argentina. Video games helped him to learn English, so now he covers them for places like The New York Times, NPR, Rolling Stone, and more. He also runs Into the Spine, a site dedicated to fostering and supporting new writers, and co-hosted Turnabout Breakdown, a podcast about the Ace Attorney series. He’s most likely playing a rhythm game as you read this.



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/xbox-and-netflix-kicking-around-ideas-for-subscription-bundles
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
